meta.title: Patient Handling (Moving & Handling of People) Assessment — Bed-to-Chair Transfer, Rehab Ward
meta.subtitle: TILE / SPHM move-toward-zero-manual-lift assessment (MHOR 1992 reg 4 / ANA SPHM 2021 / ISO/TR 12296)
meta.author: HSE Healthcare
meta.deid_notice: All personnel and patients are referred to by role label or de-identified attribute. The patient is assessed by de-identified mobility / dependency level and weight band only — no name, MRN/hospital number, ward/bay, diagnosis, or care-plan detail. The worker's capability under TILE Individual is assessed by role; any fitness / back-condition / occupational-health record is held confidentially and separately. Any handling-injury data below is de-identified and aggregated, with categories of fewer than five suppressed. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: Bed-to-chair transfer, rehab ward. The avoid-the-manual-lift decision (below) was recorded BEFORE any equipment or technique. The patient is assessed by de-identified mobility / dependency / weight band; the worker is assessed by role only; no name, ward/bay, or diagnosis is carried into this circulated assessment.

Avoid-first: the hazardous manual lift is avoided by substituting a ceiling/mobile hoist with the matched sling for the full transfer, and a slide sheet for in-bed repositioning (MHOR reg 4(1)(a) avoid; ANA SPHM / NIOSH move-toward-zero). A 'two-person manual lift' where a hoist is reasonably available, or a 'use good technique / wear a back belt' headline, is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy — technique and PPE are the documented last lines, never the control.

rows: Bed-to-chair transfer + in-bed repositioning; low-frequency per shift; static-posture hold risk during positioning; minimise twisting and reaching
Two trained handlers, hoist-competent (assessed by role; the worker's capability is recorded without any name or fitness/back-condition record in this copy)
Patient by mobility/dependency: fully dependent, limited cooperation, standard weight band; one IV line attachment — assessed by de-identified mobility/dependency, no name/diagnosis
Adequate floor space; ceiling-track hoist available over the bed; bed/chair height adjustable; clear transfer path
sections.caption: TILE assessment of the residual handling per MHOR Schedule 1 — all four elements assessed (a TILE assessment missing any element is not suitable and sufficient)

sections.text: Mobility-and-equipment matrix (dependency level → equipment + handler count)

rows: Stand-aid / sit-to-stand hoist; transfer belt (guide only)
Active/standing hoist with sling; slide sheet for repositioning
Mobile or ceiling-track passive hoist + matched sling; slide sheet
No — hoist; manual lift NOT used
Bariatric-rated hoist (verified SWL); bariatric sling; lateral-transfer aid; environmental loading checked
Mobile hoist with floor-recovery sling or inflatable lifting cushion; post-fall handling plan
sections.caption: The core artifact: each dependency level maps to the matched mechanical aid + handler count; a manual lift is NOT recommended where a mechanical aid is reasonably available (move-toward-zero)

sections.text: Hierarchy-ranked controls (eliminate manual lift → mechanical aids → safe systems of work → technique/PPE last)

rows.control: Eliminate the manual lift — ceiling/mobile hoist + matched sling for the transfer; slide sheet for repositioning
Maintained, SWL-verified hoist + sling inventory; adjustable bed/chair; ceiling-track coverage over the bed
Safe system of work for the transfer; hoist/sling competency training; the mobility-and-equipment matrix posted at point of care
Handling technique + back-care guidance as the residual last line (never the primary control); back belt only where justified

sections.text: Technique and PPE are NOT the primary control. An assessment whose headline control is 'use good technique / wear a back belt', or that recommends a manual lift where a hoist or slide aid is reasonably available, is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy (MHOR reg 4 avoid-first duty).

items: Bariatric: verify the hoist + sling safe-working-load (SWL) against the patient's weight band; check environmental floor/ceiling-track loading; allow extended planning time and additional handlers.
Falls: a post-fall handling plan uses a mechanical floor-recovery aid (mobile hoist + floor-recovery sling or inflatable lifting cushion) — never a manual lift from the floor.

metrics.label: Transfers covered by a mechanical aid
metrics.caption: post-assessment

rows.title: Manual two-person lift proposed where a ceiling hoist is available
rows.description: A draft control proposed a two-person manual lift for the full transfer; flagged and pushed up the hierarchy — the manual lift is avoided with the ceiling-track hoist (move-toward-zero).

rows.title: Back belt offered as the primary control
rows.description: A back belt was proposed as the headline control; reduced to a residual measure only — avoiding the lift and the mechanical aid are the higher-order controls.

sections.text: After avoiding the manual lift (ceiling-track hoist + slide sheet), SWL-verified equipment, the safe system of work, and competency training, the residual moving-and-handling risk is framed via risk_matrix as Low–Medium, with handling technique / back-care as the last line.

rows.action: Confirm ceiling-track hoist coverage + matched sling inventory over the transfer bed; verify SWL
Withdraw the manual-lift instruction; post the mobility-and-equipment matrix at point of care
Refresh hoist/sling competency training for the ward handlers
Verify the bariatric SWL + falls floor-recovery plan are in place and tested

items: Handling-injury counts taken from the de-identified moving-and-handling record; categories of fewer than five are suppressed — a missing figure is a [GAP], never invented.
India site: state factory/occupational-ergonomics provision owed — [GAP] (resolve the state via hse-india; no national form number minted).
UK Manual Handling Operations Regulations 1992 — reg 4(1)(a) avoid, reg 4(1)(b)(i) suitable & sufficient assessment, reg 4(1)(b)(ii) reduce, Schedule 1 TILE factors, reg 4(2) review
ANA Safe Patient Handling and Mobility (SPHM): Interprofessional National Standards, 2nd ed. (2021) — the move-toward-zero-manual-lift culture + the 8 standards
NIOSH safe-lifting guidance (minimize manual patient lifting) + ISO/TR 12296:2012 (manual handling of people in healthcare)
India factory/occupational ergonomics via hse-india (no national form number)
Review trigger: annual / on-change-of-task-or-patient / on-incident — whichever is first
Decision-support only — a competent person (moving-and-handling / ergonomics specialist) must review before use.
